title: Resolving growth-induced off-stoichiometry in AgCrSe$_2$ single crystals
authors: Felix Eder, Zeno Maesen, Yurii Skourski, Enrico Giannini, Oksana Zaharko, Fabian O. von Rohr
abstract: The layered delafossite-like antiferromagnet AgCrSe$_2$ is a superionic conductor at high temperatures and has been reported to exhibit anomalous Hall behavior and Kondo physics at low temperatures. These extraordinary transport properties have been established almost exclusively on single crystals grown by chemical vapor transport, raising questions about the role of growth-induced off-stoichiometry. Using elemental analysis, single-crystal X-ray diffraction, and magnetization measurements, we show that such crystals are indeed systematically off-stoichiometric, with a general composition of Ag$_{1-x}$Cr(Se$_{2-y}$Cl$_y$) ($x \approx y \approx 0.08$) arising from the use of CrCl$_3$ as a transport agent. This off-stoichiometry manifests in altered magnetic properties, most notably a suppressed Néel temperature of 46\,K compared to 58\,K in stoichiometric polycrystalline samples prepared by solid-state synthesis. By optimizing an Ag/Se self-flux growth method, we obtained large single crystals of AgCrSe$_2$ that recover the magnetic transition temperature and saturation field of stoichiometric powder samples. These results establish self-flux growth as a route to high-quality stoichiometric AgCrSe$_2$ single crystals and provide a reliable platform for reassessing whether the reported anomalous transport phenomena are intrinsic or arise from off-stoichiometry.
summary: This paper addresses a critical quality issue in the study of AgCrSe2 single crystals. It reveals that previous crystals grown by chemical vapor transport contain significant impurities from the transport agent, which alters their magnetic properties. By developing a new Ag/Se self-flux growth method, the researchers produced stoichiometric crystals that match the properties of bulk powder. This work provides a reliable material platform for future studies into the intrinsic anomalous Hall effect and Kondo physics in this system.
main_problem: Investigating whether the anomalous Hall behavior and Kondo physics reported in AgCrSe2 single crystals are intrinsic properties or artifacts of off-stoichiometry caused by the chemical vapor transport growth method.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that crystals grown via chemical vapor transport are systematically off-stoichiometric (Ag1-xCr(Se2-yCly)), leading to a suppressed Neel temperature; however, they successfully grew high-quality stoichiometric crystals using an Ag/Se self-flux method.
method: The study employs elemental analysis, single-crystal X-ray diffraction, and magnetization measurements to characterize composition and magnetic properties, comparing chemical vapor transport-grown crystals to self-flux grown crystals and polycrystalline samples.
model_or_system: The physical system is AgCrSe2, a layered delafossite-like antiferromagnet that acts as a superionic conductor at high temperatures and exhibits complex low-temperature transport phenomena.
key_observables: Néel temperature (Tc), saturation field, and chemical composition (stoichiometry).
important_parameters: Néel temperature (46 K for off-stoichiometric vs 58 K for stoichiometric), and the off-stoichiometry levels (x approx y approx 0.08).
paper_structure: The paper identifies the issue of off-stoichiometry in existing crystals, quantifies the chemical deviations using diffraction and elemental analysis, compares magnetic properties of different growth methods, and presents the successful recovery of stoichiometric properties via the self-flux method.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
